rollbackAssumptions: Montgomery remains not activated and not onboarded.
V579 performs no runtime rollback and executes no migration.
Rollback readiness is evaluated from package-scoped documentary artifacts only.
Expected V577 registry and package-manifest paths are dependency references; absent files remain observations in this workspace.
validationRationale: Rollback readiness is complete because a documented procedure, verification checklist, ownership expectations, communication expectations, review requirements, and protected-system safeguards are present. Registry and package-manifest dependency files are documented as observations because the expected V577 files are not present in this workspace.
rollbackReadinessRationale: The package can support a future activation readiness decision by defining how to pause progression, verify Montgomery package scope, confirm dependencies, and preserve protected systems without executing runtime rollback.
reversibilityExpectations: V579 artifacts are documentation-only and can be reviewed or superseded without changing runtime systems.
Future activation work must remain paused if dependency observations are unresolved.
Any future production registry, Supabase, migration, data placement, or activation rollback must be authorized by a separate milestone.
